article_meta.title: The three-way battle between Democrats running for Senate in Illinois has evolved into an argument about the best way to

reasoning: The model correctly identifies the article as Lean Left with good reasoning. The key_indicators cite the author's own prose, not quoted sources. The confidence of 0.8 is reasonable for the level of bias signals present. Minor issues: some of the key_indicators include phrases that are borderline between author framing and neutral description, but they are legitimately the author's own editorial choices (e.g., 'combat President Trump's deportation agenda' is the author's framing, 'dismantling Mr. Trump's immigration enforcement structure' is the author's characterization). The classification is defensible — the article covers a Democratic primary through a lens sympathetic to anti-ICE positions without presenting Republican counterarguments.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Lean Left is the correct classification for this NYT news article. The author frames the entire piece around Democratic approaches to opposing Trump's immigration enforcement, with no Republican perspective included. The structural choices (source selection, framing, omission) all tilt left. Lean Left is exactly right — not Left because the language isn't strongly loaded.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.8 is reasonable for this article. The bias signals are clear through framing and omission but the author doesn't use heavily loaded language. A confidence of 0.75-0.85 would be appropriate; 0.8 fits well.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: All four key_indicators cite the author's own prose. 'Combat President Trump's deportation agenda' is the author's framing choice. 'Dismantling Mr. Trump's immigration enforcement structure' is the author's characterization. 'Democratic anger' is the author's description. The fourth indicator about 'ambitious Democrats' is also the author's own analysis. No attribution violations. The reasoning correctly identifies structural bias through omission and framing.
